mission.title: Install and Configure Volcano on Kubernetes
mission.description: Volcano is a Kubernetes-native batch scheduling system that enhances the capabilities of the standard kube-scheduler. It is designed to manage and optimize various batch and elastic workloads, making it ideal for AI, Big Data, and HPC applications.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Volcano Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add kubegems https://charts.kubegems.io/kubegems
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Volcano using Helm
steps.description:
Install Volcano in the 'volcano-system' namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install volcano kubegems/volcano --namespace volcano-system --create-namespace --version 1.12.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Volcano pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n volcano-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Volcano components to optimize performance.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment volcano-controller --namespace volcano-system --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Volcano release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall volcano --namespace volcano-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by Volcano.
```bash
kubectl delete crd volcans.volcano.sh
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'volcano-system' namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace volcano-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of Volcano before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get all -n volcano-system -o yaml > volcano-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Volcano
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Volcano installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade volcano kubegems/volcano --namespace volcano-system --version 1.12.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Volcano pods to ensure they are running correctly after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n volcano-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability and scheduling issues.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n volcano-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Volcano controller not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Volcano controller pod fails to start, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <volcano-controller-pod-name> -n volcano-system
```

troubleshooting.title: High resource usage
troubleshooting.description:
Monitor resource usage to identify if any component is consuming excessive resources.
```bash
kubectl top pods -n volcano-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Volcano will have all pods running in the 'volcano-system' namespace without errors. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and ensuring they are in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubegems https://charts.kubegems.io/kubegems
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl.
